category_title: Extremely shy students with social engagement difficulties
minimal_context: The student is using drawing software during an alternative lunch time session to create a digital picture depicting fair play and honesty in rugby league.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time to create your digital picture using the drawing software. Please make a visual representation showing your ideas about fairness and honesty, like 'no cheating' in rugby league.

STUDENT: (Stares at the blank screen on the drawing software, shrinking back slightly in her chair and remaining completely silent in the peer group setting.)

TUTOR: Go ahead and start drawing your picture.

STUDENT: (Keeps her hands in her lap, looking down anxiously and avoiding any interaction with the other students at the table.)

TUTOR: You need to finish your designated work before this lunch time option is over.

STUDENT: (Pushes the mouse away, crosses her arms, and turns her body away from the computer, exhibiting complete work refusal and high anxiety.)

options.A: Interweave rugby league player narratives of their daily lives and work into the curriculum before and after their weekly visits.
options.B: Provide more time for the alternative lunch time sessions, extending the drawing activity to 30 minutes rather than 15.
options.C: Show the digital icon of each peer group member on the same page of the drawing software during the session.
options.D: Use physical objects as figurative media for the drawing task, incorporating items such as images, posters, and rugby balls.
